title: Cloning with NEB Instant Sticky-end Ligase Master Mix (M0370)
author: Harold Bien
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.d659g5

steps.title: Prepare Ligase Master Mix
steps.instruction: Place master mix tube on ice and flick a few times to mix.

steps.title: Prepare Ligation Reaction
steps.instruction: Combine 20–100 ng of vector with a 3-fold molar excess of insert and q.s. to 5μl with dH2O.

steps.title: Add Ligase and Mix
steps.instruction: Add 5 μl of Instant Sticky-end Ligase Master Mix, mix thoroughly by pipetting up and down 7-10 times, and place on ice. The sample is now ready to be used for transformation.

steps.title: Aliquot Competent Cells
steps.instruction: Aliquot 50 μl of cells into a 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Add Ligation Reaction to Cells
steps.instruction: Add 2 μl of the ligation reaction to the cells and mix by finger-flicking. Do not vortex the tube.

steps.title: Ice Incubation
steps.instruction: Incubate the tube on ice for 30 minutes. Do not mix.

steps.title: Return to Ice
steps.instruction: Return tube to ice for 2 minutes.

steps.title: Add Recovery Media
steps.instruction: Add 950 μl recovery media (e.g. SOC) to the tube.

steps.title: Recovery Incubation
steps.instruction: Incubate for one hour at 37°C with rotation or shaking (200–250 rpm).

steps.title: Plate and Incubate
steps.instruction: Spread 100 μl of the outgrowth (undiluted or diluted 1:5 with recovery media) onto appropriate antibiotic selection plates and incubate overnight at 37°C.
